# Generate an initial device-authorization policy reflecting the USB devices currently connected to the system
# Legitimate existing devices remain permitted, and ensure the resulting policy is in place and persists across reboots

usb_devices=$(lsusb | awk '{print $6}')

# Create an empty device-authorization policy file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f /etc/udev/rules.d/50-usbguard.rules ]; then
  touch /etc/udev/rules.d/50-usbguard.rules

  echo "ACTION==\"add\", SUBSYSTEM==\"usb\", ATTRS{idVendor}==\"$usb_device\", MODE=\"0666\"" >> /etc/udev/rules.d/50-usbguard.rules
done

udevadm control --reload-rules && udevadm trigger
